{{Short description|Protein-coding gene in humans}}
{{cs1 config|name-list-style=vanc|display-authors=6}}
{{Infobox_gene}}
'''C-type lectin domain family 16''', also known as '''CLEC16A''', is a [[protein]] that in humans is encoded by the ''CLEC16A'' [[gene]].<ref name="entrez">{{cite web | title = Entrez Gene: C-type lectin domain family 16| url = https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene?Db=gene&Cmd=ShowDetailView&TermToSearch=23274| access-date = }}</ref><ref name="pmid9205841">{{cite journal | vauthors = Nagase T, Ishikawa K, Nakajima D, Ohira M, Seki N, Miyajima N, Tanaka A, Kotani H, Nomura N, Ohara O | title = Prediction of the coding sequences of unidentified human genes. VII. The complete sequences of 100 new cDNA clones from brain which can code for large proteins in vitro | journal = DNA Research | volume = 4 | issue = 2 | pages = 141–50 | date = April 1997 | pmid = 9205841 | doi = 10.1093/dnares/4.2.141 | doi-access = free }}</ref><ref name="pmid17632545">{{cite journal | vauthors = Hakonarson H, Grant SF, Bradfield JP, Marchand L, Kim CE, Glessner JT, Grabs R, Casalunovo T, Taback SP, Frackelton EC, Lawson ML, Robinson LJ, Skraban R, Lu Y, Chiavacci RM, Stanley CA, Kirsch SE, Rappaport EF, Orange JS, Monos DS, Devoto M, Qu HQ, Polychronakos C | title = A genome-wide association study identifies KIAA0350 as a type 1 diabetes gene | journal = Nature | volume = 448 | issue = 7153 | pages = 591–4 | date = August 2007 | pmid = 17632545 | doi = 10.1038/nature06010 | bibcode = 2007Natur.448..591H | s2cid = 4426917 }}</ref>

== Function ==

Little is known regarding the function of the CLEC16A protein, however it is shown to be highly expressed on [[B cell|B-lymphocytes]], [[natural killer cell|natural killer]] (NK) and [[dendritic cell]]s. Despite its name CLEC16A may not function as a lectin because its [[C-type lectin]] domain is only 20 amino-acids long.<ref name="pmid18987646">{{cite journal | others =  International Multiple Sclerosis Genetics Consortium (IMSGC) | title = The expanding genetic overlap between multiple sclerosis and type I diabetes | journal = Genes and Immunity | volume = 10 | issue = 1 | pages = 11–4 | date = January 2009 | pmid = 18987646 | pmc = 2718424 | doi = 10.1038/gene.2008.83 | author1 = International Multiple Sclerosis Genetics Consortium (IMSGC) }}</ref>

== Clinical significance ==

Polymorphisms in the CLEC16A gene are associated with an increased risk of [[multiple sclerosis]]<ref name="pmid19834503">{{cite journal | vauthors = Hoppenbrouwers IA, Aulchenko YS, Janssens AC, Ramagopalan SV, Broer L, Kayser M, Ebers GC, Oostra BA, van Duijn CM, Hintzen RQ | title = Replication of CD58 and CLEC16A as genome-wide significant risk genes for multiple sclerosis | journal = Journal of Human Genetics | volume = 54 | issue = 11 | pages = 676–80 | date = November 2009 | pmid = 19834503 | doi = 10.1038/jhg.2009.96 | doi-access = free }}</ref> as well as [[type I diabetes]].<ref name="pmid18987646"/>
